hook.text: Jim Benson scaled Kanban down to one person: a sticky-note board for your own work.

buildup.text: Three columns — To Do, Doing, Done — with a personal WIP limit of two or three items.

discovery.text: Visualizing your own work reveals how much you juggle — and why nothing ever feels finished.

twist.text: Personal Kanban works for shopping lists, home projects, and even managing a job search.

climax.text: The same system that runs Toyota factories can organize one person's Tuesday.

punchline.text: Three columns and a WIP limit. That's all you need.

quiz.question: What is the key rule in Personal Kanban?
options: Limit work in progress to just a few items at a time
Complete everything on your list by Friday
Never say no to new tasks
